title: Pentacalia hitchcockii
intro: Pentacalia hitchcockii is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the cloud forests of Ecuador. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health issues, such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia hitchcockii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pentacalia hitchcockii is traditionally used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from fever and colds to skin conditions and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pentacalia hitchcockii is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia and is a member of the tribe Senecioneae. The species is classified under the kingdom Plantae, and its classification is further refined by its position within the clade Angiosperms, then Eudicots, and finally Asterales. Pentacalia hitchcockii is native to Ecuador. Its classification is also influenced by its evolutionary relationships with other species in the same family and genus.
